# Failed Task Rebuild Convention

## What I'm Seeing

`tasks/failed/seed-skill-gaps.md` scored 5/10 — the spec was correct and the content quality was good, but 11 of 14 skills exceeded the spec's 40-line hard constraint. The failure report provides a clear path to resolution ("Builder should trim each of the 11 failing skills to ≤40 lines"). The learning has been extracted to `learnings/failures/2026-02-24-seed-skill-gaps-verification-failure.md`.

The `failure-learning-convention` spec (`specs/archive/failure-learning-convention.md`) defines a re-verification path: manually move a task from `tasks/failed/` to `tasks/review/` for fresh verification. But re-verification only checks existing artifacts — it won't produce different results when the artifacts themselves need to be rebuilt. The seed-skill-gaps task needs the builder to redo the work, not the verifier to re-check it.

There is no defined convention for returning a spec to `specs/ready/` after a build-quality failure so the builder can rebuild. The spec sits in `specs/archive/` (correctly specced), the failed verification sits in `tasks/failed/` (correctly identified), and the 14 seed skills remain unbuilt. The pipeline has no mechanism to close this loop.

## What I Want to See

A convention for the rebuild recovery path when a task fails verification due to build quality (not blockers or spec issues). The convention should define: who initiates the rebuild (human or runtime), what file movement signals "rebuild needed" vs "re-verify only", and whether the prior failure report is preserved or replaced. The immediate effect should be unblocking the seed-skill-gaps spec for rebuild so the 14 seed skills can be completed within spec constraints.
